1.title: Date and Time
1.description: The searchable date/time of the data, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

1.title: Description
1.description: Detailed multi-line description to fully explain the entity.

1.title: End Date and Time
1.description: The searchable end date/time of the data, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

1.title: Keywords
1.description: List of keywords describing the entity.

1.title: Start Date and Time
1.description: The searchable start date/time of the data, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

1.title: Total number of all data values
Maximum value of all the data values
Mean value of all the data values
Minimum value of all the data values
Standard deviation value of all the data values
Title
1.description: A human-readable title describing the entity.

1.title: Unit denomination of the data value
Collection ID
1.description: The ID of the STAC Collection this Item references to.

properties.title: End Date and Time
properties.description: The searchable end date/time of the assets, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

properties.title: Start Date and Time
properties.description: The searchable start date/time of the assets, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

properties.title: Date and Time
properties.description: The searchable date/time of the assets, in UTC (Formatted in RFC 3339)

properties.title: Asset description
properties.description: Detailed multi-line description to fully explain the Item.

properties.title: Asset title
properties.description: A human-readable title describing the Item.

properties.title: Item links
properties.description: Links to item relations

properties.title: Collection ID
properties.description: The ID of the STAC Collection this Item references to.

properties.title: Provider ID
properties.description: Provider item ID

This object contains one key per substitution variable in the templated URL.  Each key defines the schema of one substitution variable using a JSON Schema fragment and can thus include things like the data type of the variable, enumerations, minimum values, maximum values, etc.
The base URI to which the variable name can be appended to retrieve the definition of the variable as a JSON Schema fragment.
Supplies a resolvable URI to a remote resource (or resource fragment).
Minimum time period resolvable in the dataset, as an ISO 8601 duration
Most recent date on which the resource pointed to by the link was changed.
Date of creation of the resource pointed to by the link.

properties.title: Link title
properties.description: Used to label the destination of a link such that it can be used as a human-readable identifier.
A hint indicating what the language of the result of dereferencing the link should be.

properties.title: Link type
properties.description: A hint indicating what the media type of the result of dereferencing the link should be.

properties.title: Link relation type
properties.description: The type or semantics of the relation.
A statement that concerns all rights not addressed by the license such as a copyright statement.
A list of contacts qualified by their role(s) in association to the record or the resource described by the record.
The set of named duties, job functions and/or permissions associated with this contact. (e.g. developer, administrator, etc.).

Supplemental instructions on how or when to contact the
responsible party.
Time period when the contact can be contacted.
On-line information about the contact.
Physical location at which contact can be made.
The type of address (e.g. office, home, etc.).
Country of the physical address.  ISO 3166-1 is recommended.
ZIP or other postal code.
State or province of the location.
City for the location.
Address lines for the location.
Email addresses at which contact can be made.
The type of email (e.g. home, work, etc.).
The value is the email number itself.
Telephone numbers at which contact can be made.
The type of phone number (e.g. home, work, fax, etc.).
The value is the phone number itself.
Graphic identifying a contact. The link relation should be `icon` and the media type should be an image media type.
Organization/affiliation of the contact.
The name of the role or position of the responsible person taken from the organization's formal organizational hierarchy or chart.
The name of the responsible person.
A value uniquely identifying a contact.
A list of available distributions of the resource.
A knowledge organization system used to classify the resource.

An identifier for the knowledge organization system used
to classify the resource.  It is recommended that the
identifier be a resolvable URI.  The list of schemes used
in a searchable catalog can be determined by inspecting
the server's OpenAPI document or, if the server implements
CQL2, by exposing a queryable (e.g. named `scheme`) and
enumerating the list of schemes in the queryable's schema
definition.

One or more entity/concept identifiers from this knowledge
system. it is recommended that a resolvable URI be used for
each entity/concept identifier.
A URI providing further description of the concept.
A human readable description for the concept.
A human readable title for the concept.
An identifier for the concept.
An identifier for the resource assigned by an external (to the catalog) entity.
The value of the identifier.
A reference to an authority or identifier for a knowledge organization system from which the external identifier was obtained. It is recommended that the identifier be a resolvable URI.
The list of languages in which the resource described by this record is available.
This list of languages in which this record is available.
The language used for textual values in this record representation.

The direction for text in this language. The default, `ltr`
(left-to-right), represents the most common situation.
However, care should be taken to set the value of `dir`
appropriately if the language direction is not `ltr`.
Other values supported are `rtl` (right-to-left), `ttb`
(top-to-bottom), and `btt` (bottom-to-top).

The name of the language in another well-understood language,
usually English.
The untranslated name of the language.
The language tag as per RFC-5646.
The topic or topics of the resource. Typically represented using free-form keywords, tags, key phrases, or classification codes.
A free-text account of the resource.
A human-readable name given to the resource.

The nature or genre of the resource. The value should be a code, convenient for filtering records. Where available, a link to the canonical URI of the record type resource will be added to the 'links' property.
The most recent date on which the record was changed.
Date of creation of this record.

properties.title: Provider ID
properties.description: A unique identifier of the catalog record.
